title.en: 改变我如何看待速拧的一个顿悟 -- 左手公式右手化

content.en:
改变我如何看待速拧的一个顿悟 -- 左手公式右手化

Jayden McNeill

作为一个右撇子魔友, 我做左手的解法和公式从来都不顺手.

理想情况下, 我们应该全部都是双撇子 (ambidextrous), 但是无论如何, 肯定会有一只手相对来说更协调一点...

完美的平衡不可能存在, 也不实用.

尽管如此, 左手的情况还是很多, 而且在2018年的一段时间里, 我甚至没有意识到不能快速地做左手公式无法让我变得更快.

当Michal Rzewuski指出我的LU转动比RU差很多时, 这种状况发生了改变.

我很快发现他说得完全正确, 所以我将我弥补转动时出现的明显缺陷视为我的一项任务.

在经过了几个星期的针对性练习后, 我的百次PB (PB, Personal Best) 时间变短了, 并且经过了几个月后...

...我获得了一个平均Sub 7的个人纪录 (PR, Personal Record), 并且至今仍是我唯一一个官方Sub 7平均 (其实不久我就不专注练习它了, 并且开始教学/内容/写电子报, 而且过了一段时间我已准备好再多参加一些比赛时, 新冠肺炎爆发了哈哈).

那么, 我到底是如何纠正我的左手转动的呢?

本质上, 我给自己 "洗脑" , 强行将自己将左手解法视为右手解法.

举个例子, 与其做L' U2 L U' L' U L且想着我在做一个LU流的F2L情况, 而不如将其想作r' F2 r U' r' F r并且假装那个公式实际是rUF流的.

我知道, 这听起来荒谬可笑, 但是我希望你尝试一下 (这最多需要三分钟的时间).

你准备好了吗?

...

好了

拿出任何一个计时器

现在, 想着像做一个仅有LU的转动序列 (字符串) 一样做L' U L U' L' U L U2' L' U L, 然后在计时器上看看当你想着它是L和U转动时能做到多快.

一旦做好了并尝试了若干次, 尝试测个比较快的5次平均.

5次平均?

现在, 做和刚才一样的所有事情, 唯一的不同点是, 想着做r' F r U' r' F r U2' r' F r并且真正感觉着你的右手在做r和r' 转动.

尝试并集中注意力在你用得惯的手上, 再来把快的5次平均.

这样一来, 我就有机会神奇地 "解决" 你的 "左手问题" ;).

我做的另一件事情 (如果你想在此方面更上一层楼的话), 你还应该将左手公式的还原时间和与其镜像的右手公式的还原时间进行比较 (如果你不会做镜像公式, 那么请用这个 [http://cube.rider.biz/algtrans.html] 的 "Mirror L/R").

比方说, L' U L U' L' U L U2' L' U' L用了你0.97秒, 但是R U' R' U R U' R' U2 R U' R'却只用了你0.66秒.

现在, 你就有了一个针对LU流公式的好目标.

-J

词汇

epiphany 顿悟

ambidextrous 双撇子

The Epiphany That Changed How I See Speedsolving

Jayden McNeill – https://www.jaydenmcneillcubing.com/

As a right hand dominant solver, left handed solutions & algorithms never felt as natural for me

 

Ideally we would all be ambidextrous, but no matter what, one side is always going to be a little more coordinated...

 

Perfect balance isn't possible, nor is it practical

 

Still, lefty cases come up a lot, and for a period of time back in 2018, not being able to do them quickly was holding me back without even realizing it...

 

This changed when Michal Rzewuski pointed out that my LU turning was a LOT worse than my RU turning

 

I figured out pretty quickly that he was right on the money, and I made it my mission to correct this glaring weakness in my turning

 

After working on it for a few weeks, my personal best avg100 dropped, and a few months after that...

 

...I got a sub 7 official 3x3 average PR, which still to this day stands as my only sub 7 official average (shortly after I stopped focusing on practice and instead on coaching/content/the newsletter, and by the time I was ready to compete more again, Covid happened lol)

 

So, what exactly did I do to fix my lefty turning?

 

 

Essentially, I "brainwashed" myself to see lefty solutions as righty solutions

 

 

For example, instead of doing L' U2 L U' L' U L and thinking of the pair as LU gen, think about it as r' F2 r U' r' F r and pretend that the algorithm is actually rUF gen

 

I know, this sounds so basic as ridiculous, but I want you to try something for me (it'll take 3 mins tops)

 

You ready kid?

 

...

 

Alright

 

Take out a timer, any timer

 

Now, think about doing L' U L U' L' U L U2' L' U L as only a string of L and U moves, and see how fast you can get it on a timer while thinking about it as L and U turns 

 

Once you've done that and timed it a few times, try and string together a fast avg5 while thinking about the L and U turns

 

Got the avg5?

 

Now, do the same thing again except this time think about doing r' F r U' r' F r U2' r' F r and really FEEL the r and r' turns in your right hand

 

Again, try and string together a fast avg5 while focusing on the dominant hand

 

There's a chance that I just magically "fixed" your "left hand problems" ;)

 

Another thing I did (If you wanna take this to the next level), you should also compare your lefty solution times with the righty equivalents times (use this and the Mirror L/R section if you get stuck)

 

Let's say L' U L U' L' U L U2' L' U L takes you .97 of a second, but R U' R' U R U' R' U2 R U' R' takes you .66 of a second

 

Now you have a nice goal to shoot for with the LU alg

-J
